title: if Statement
instruction: An `if` runs a block only when the condition is true. Check if `x` (set to 15) is greater than 10; if so, print "greater than 10".

title: if-else
instruction: Use `if-else` to run different code by condition. If score (72) is 60 or higher print "Pass", otherwise print "Fail".

title: if with Init Statement
instruction: Go lets you run a short statement before the condition in an `if`. Look up the key "go" in the map `langs`. If it exists print "found", else "not found".
hint: if v, ok := langs["go"]; ok { fmt.Println("found:", v) } else { fmt.Println("not found") }

title: switch Statement
instruction: Use a `switch` on `day` ("Monday") to print "Weekday" for Monday–Friday and "Weekend" for Saturday–Sunday.
hint: switch day { case "Monday", "Tuesday", ...: fmt.Println("Weekday") case "Saturday", "Sunday": fmt.Println("Weekend") }

title: FizzBuzz
instruction: Loop from 1 to 15. For multiples of 3 print "Fizz", of 5 print "Buzz", of both print "FizzBuzz", else print the number. Check FizzBuzz first.
hint: Check i%15==0 first for FizzBuzz, then i%3==0 for Fizz, then i%5==0 for Buzz, else print i.
